overview: A thin orchestration layer for Cursor agents providing long-term memory, module ownership, and multi-agent coordination. It utilizes .mdc files for modular memory, a write-back loop for learning, and a RALPH-style iterative engine to ensure task completion through testing and architectural consistency.

requirements.title: Module Ownership Registry
requirements.description: Establish a system to map specific code modules to dedicated Agent Teams to maintain architectural consistency and contextual expertise.
success_criteria: Registry accurately maps file paths to agent IDs using longest-prefix matching.
System prevents a single file from having more than one primary owner.
Changes to ownership are persisted in a git-trackable .ace/ownership.yaml file.

sub_requirements.title: Ownership CLI
sub_requirements.description: CLI tools to assign, query, and list owners (ace own, ace who, ace list-owners).
success_criteria: CLI returns the correct owner for a nested file based on its parent directory ownership.

requirements.title: Dynamic Context Builder
requirements.description: Compose a specialized 'Memory Slice' for every agent call by combining global rules, agent-specific playbooks, recent ADRs, and session history.
success_criteria: Context composition logic correctly prioritizes .mdc files based on the target module.
Injected context includes the 3 most recent relevant architectural decisions (ADRs).
Prompt-prefixing varies correctly based on task type (implement, review, debug, refactor, plan).

requirements.title: Write-back Learning Pipeline
requirements.description: An automated reflection process where agents extract lessons learned, new strategies, and pitfalls after completing a task and update their .mdc playbooks.
success_criteria: Orchestrator successfully extracts delta-updates in JSON format from agent output.
The .mdc files are updated incrementally without losing existing structure or metadata.
Helpful/Harmful counters for strategies are correctly incremented based on task outcomes.

requirements.title: RALPH Loop Engine
requirements.description: Implement a Reasoning, Action, Learning, Progress, Halt cycle to iteratively run the agent until defined tests pass.
success_criteria: Engine terminates execution when user-defined tests pass or max_iterations is reached.
Each iteration refreshes context with learnings captured from the previous failed attempt.
Failed test logs are fed back into the 'Reasoning' phase of the next iteration.

requirements.title: Multi-Agent Coordination & Consensus
requirements.description: Facilitate communication and architectural debate between different specialized agents when tasks cross ownership boundaries.
success_criteria: Agent Mail system allows agents to send and receive markdown-based messages in .ace/mail/.
Consensus Protocol triggers when a task affects modules owned by different agents.
LLM-referee or Arch-agent can resolve or escalate debates to human users after 3 rounds.

sub_requirements.title: Token Consumption Modes
sub_requirements.description: Control operational costs by limiting context and debate depth (Low, Medium, High).
success_criteria: Low mode restricts context to single-task focus with no async debate.
High mode enables proactive refactoring suggestions and deep context analysis.

requirements.title: UI/UX Orchestration (Google Stitch)
requirements.description: Integrate Google Stitch to enable AI-driven design mockups and code generation within the ACE workflow.
success_criteria: ACE can generate a 'Vibe Design' prompt for Google Stitch.
CLI command 'ace ui sync' successfully imports code (Tailwind/Flutter) from a Stitch canvas URL.

requirements.title: Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs)
requirements.description: Implement formal protocols for agent onboarding, PR reviews, and subsystem audits.
success_criteria: Onboarding SOP generates a markdown summary of technical debt and subsystem status.
Cross-agent PR reviews identify deviations from the agent's playbook strategies.

requirements.title: Agent Memory Management
requirements.description: Maintain a central AGENTS.md for cross-tool visibility and provide tools for memory pruning.
success_criteria: AGENTS.md is updated automatically to reflect the current agent registry and roles.
The 'ace memory prune' command successfully removes strategies where 'harmful' counts exceed 'helpful' counts.
